anonymization.note: All EDF timestamps shifted by a fixed offset. Real serial replaced with fixture placeholder. No real patient data is identifiable.

notes: Fixture generated by scrub_sdcard.py from a contributor-owned AirSense 10 card. Timestamps shifted; serial replaced. Suitable for cpap-parser conformance testing.
import_expected_provenance: The semantic expected.import blocks (warnings/session_blocks/therapy_aggregates/events/settings) below are NOT hand-authored: each value was produced by the actual normalized ImportRun from ResMedNativeLoader.import_data_with_directory on this committed fixture (default ImportOptions, no waveforms), parsed with cpap-parser[resmed]+cpap-py in a Linux environment. Dates are the anonymized (timestamp_shift_days=-508) machine-local dates the parser emits from the committed STR.edf — non-identifying by construction. Only non-timestamped aggregates are pinned: warning codes, per-night block counts, usage/wall-clock/gap seconds, event counts, and the device-reported therapy mode (settings.therapy_mode, from cpap-parser pressure_mode — the only setting the parser exposes; APAP for this AutoSet device, non-private). No min/max/set pressure, EPR, ramp, humidifier, or mask_type (not in the cpap-parser schema), no exact block-interval or event timestamps, no serial/PHI. See docs/sleeplab_2_resmed_normalized_output_gap_audit.md.

files.note: Integrity pin for the committed, anonymized per-session OSCAR export (twin of summary.csv). sha256 of a redistributable file exposes no serial/PHI.

oscar_reference.note: Integrity pin for the committed, anonymized OSCAR export. sha256 of the redistributable summary.csv exposes no serial/PHI (plan section 11). Verified parser-free by validate_import's oscar_reference hash check; numeric parity stays a skip until a normalized run is available.
